heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
### Numeric thresholds (placeholder values)
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ X points (MCID; X = TBD)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni correction applied to secondary endpoints (≤N comparisons; N = TBD) with α = 0.025 per test
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ TBD per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >TBD% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N = TBD (TBD per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = TBD). If conditional power <TBD%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (single placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: TBD; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time)
- **Pre-registration date:** TBD (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Planned registration date:** TBD
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** Between-group difference in PAM-13 change score (Δ) from baseline to TBD months
- **Analysis plan:** Independent two-sample t-test (two-sided); ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity
- **Secondary outcomes:** Medication adherence (MMAS-8), quality of life (EQ-5D-5L), healthcare utilisation (number of hospitalisations)
- **Exploratory outcomes:** Patient activation trajectory (mixed-effects model), LLM engagement metrics (session count, message length)

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
- **Formula:** n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** Z_α/2 = 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-sided), Z_β = 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- **Effect size δ:** TBD points (PAM-13 MCID; placeholder)
- **Standard deviation σ:** TBD (assumed — see justification below)
- **Calculation:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2 · σ² / δ² = 7.84 · 2σ² / δ² → n = TBD per group
- **Total N:** TBD + TBD% dropout = TBD participants
- **Sensitivity analysis (required N per group):**
  - σ = TBD: n ≈ TBD
  - σ = TBD: n ≈ TBD (base case)
  - σ = TBD: n ≈ TBD
- **Justification for σ = TBD:** TBD

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment, extended enrollment period |
| High dropout rate | Medium | High | Retention strategies (reminders, incentives) |
| Technical failure of LLM | Low | High | Redundant systems, fallback to standard care |
| Adverse events | Low | High | DSMB monitoring, stopping rule |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Very High | Encryption, access controls, GDPR compliance |
| Confounding by site effect | Medium | Medium | Stratified randomisation, site as covariate |
| Measurement bias (PAM-13) | Low | Medium | Blinded outcome assessment, standardised training

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-centre design may limit generalisability
- PAM-13 is a self-reported measure, subject to social desirability bias
- LLM intervention is a black box; interpretability of model decisions is limited
- Placebo effect cannot be fully controlled due to lack of blinding for intervention
- Sample size calculation based on assumed effect size; true effect may differ
- Follow-up duration may be insufficient to detect long-term outcomes
- Potential for contamination between arms if participants interact
- Use of placeholder values (e.g., OSF ID, effect size) indicates incomplete specification
